perturbation.original_text: 1.53 Molecular Information Platform Program The term "Molecular Information Platform Program" shall mean the program designed to generate insights for certain of Roche's clinical development portfolio from FMI's molecular information platform, comprised of tumor sample genomic analysis, database access, and dedicated FMI clinical and genomic expertise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.53 Molecular Information Platform Program The term "Molecular Information Platform Program" shall mean the program designed to generate insights for certain of Roche's clinical development portfolio from FMI's genomic analysis system, comprised of tumor sample genomic analysis, database access, and dedicated FMI clinical and genomic expertise.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'molecular information platform' to 'genomic analysis system' introduces ambiguity. While seemingly similar, the contract doesn't define 'genomic analysis system,' leading to potential disputes over the program's scope. This change introduces a contradiction because the contract relies on the precise definition of “Molecular Information Platform” later on, and the introduction of a new term will cause uncertainty on the true scope and meaning of 1.53. This introduces a perturbation as to the legal obligations of the parties with respect to the platform.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1.4 Sample Profiling and Reserved Capacity FMI shall provide, and shall reserve capacity to provide, Roche with comprehensive profiling, analysis and reporting ("Sample Profiling") of at least […***…] during the first […***…] immediately following the Effective date, and at least […***…] samples during the next […***…] ("Reserved Capacity") using FMI's platform for molecular genomic profiling of cancer samples (the "Genomic Analysis Platform"). The initial laboratory and computational biology activities performed on the Samples as part of Sample Profiling are "Genomic Analyses". FMI will provide Sample Profiling in accordance with the Reserved Capacity during the Profiling Term and Profiling Renewal Term, using the then-current versions of the tests included in its Genomic Analysis Platform. The Sampling Profiles shall be provided in a specified format to be mutually agreed by the Parties. The JMC will discuss and decide upon the Reserved Capacity commitment for Profiling Renewal Terms, provided that any Reserved Capacity amount in excess of […***…] that would require FMI to increase its existing capacity will require FMI approval. Sample Profiling will include advanced genomic analyses, i.e. advanced laboratory and computational biology activities in the field of cancer genomic sequencing and analysis performed on Samples, including […***…] (collectively, "Advanced Genomic Analyses"). Sample Profiling includes Genomic Analyses and Advanced Genomic Analyses. FMI may adopt modifications to the Performance Specifications without Roche's consent, so long as such modifications do not result in a material diminution in the analytical performance of the Genomic Analysis Platform as measured by the metrics set forth in Appendix 1.57 (which such material diminution would require the prior written consent of Roche). In the event […***…], and such change results in a material diminution in the analytical performance of the Genomic Analysis Platform as measured by the metrics set forth in Appendix 1.57, Roche may, at its election, and upon written notice to FMI, terminate each Party's obligations under Reserved Capacity, including Roche's obligations under Section 8.2.1.1 to pay Reserved Capacity Fees and opt out of the price per Sample fees for Sample Profiling otherwise specified in Section 8.2.1.2, in each case, from the effect of such sequencing platform change. If Roche elects to terminate the Parties' obligations under Reserved Capacity and opts out of the per Sample pricing for Sample Profiling specified in Section 8.2.1.2, Sample Profiling shall then be performed, and fees for such Sample Profiling shall then be charged, on a per Sample basis at FMI's standard rates or on pricing terms to be mutually agreed in writing by the Parties (or as otherwise mutually agreed in writing by the Parties).
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the defined term "Molecular Information Platform Program" with the undefined term "genomic analysis system". The contradicted text, Section 3.1.4, uses the term "Genomic Analysis Platform". While seemingly similar, the changed definition in 1.53 makes it unclear whether "genomic analysis system" is equivalent to "Genomic Analysis Platform", or whether they are different systems. Section 3.1.4 also refers to "FMI's platform for molecular genomic profiling of cancer samples (the 'Genomic Analysis Platform')". This creates ambiguity and potential disputes regarding the scope of FMI's obligations under Section 3.1.4 and whether they apply to the changed definition of the program. The 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation definition applies directly, as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 1.17 Confidential Information The term "Confidential Information" shall mean any and all information, data or know-how (including Know-How), whether technical or non- technical, oral or written, that is disclosed by one Party or its Affiliates ("Disclosing Party") to the other Party or its Affiliates ("Receiving Party"). Confidential Information shall not include any information, data or know-how that: (i) was generally available to the public at the time of disclosure, or information that becomes available to the public after disclosureby the Disclosing Party other than through fault (whether by action or inaction) of the Receiving Party or its Affiliates, (ii) can be evidenced by written records to have been already known to the Receiving Party or its Affiliates prior to its receipt fromthe Disclosing Party, (iii) is obtained by the Receiving Party at any time lawfully from a Third Party under circumstances permitting its use or disclosure, (iv) is developed independently by the Receiving Party or its Affiliates as evidenced by written records other than throughknowledge of Confidential Information, or (v) is approved in writing by the Disclosing Party for release by the Receiving Party. The terms of this Agreement shall be considered Confidential Information of the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.17 Confidential Information The term "Confidential Information" shall mean any and all information, data or know-how (including Know-How), whether technical or non- technical, oral or written, that is disclosed by one Party or its Affiliates ("Disclosing Party") to the other Party or its Affiliates ("Receiving Party"). Proprietary Information shall not include any information, data or know-how that: (i) was generally available to the public at the time of disclosure, or information that becomes available to the public after disclosureby the Disclosing Party other than through fault (whether by action or inaction) of the Receiving Party or its Affiliates, (ii) can be evidenced by written records to have been already known to the Receiving Party or its Affiliates prior to its receipt fromthe Disclosing Party, (iii) is obtained by the Receiving Party at any time lawfully from a Third Party under circumstances permitting its use or disclosure, (iv) is developed independently by the Receiving Party or its Affiliates as evidenced by written records other than throughknowledge of Confidential Information, or (v) is approved in writing by the Disclosing Party for release by the Receiving Party. The terms of this Agreement shall be considered Confidential Information of the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing "Confidential Information" with "Proprietary Information" in the exclusion clause, the contract creates confusion. It's now unclear whether the exceptions apply to all Confidential Information as defined or only to information explicitly labeled as 'Proprietary Information'. This leads to uncertainty in enforcement regarding the scope of confidentiality. This creates an in-text contradiction because in the original text, all the 5 bullet points were intended to be excluded for the definition of “Confidential Information”.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16.1 Non-Use and Non-Disclosure During the Agreement Term and for […***…] thereafter, a Receiving Party shall (i) treat Confidential Information provided by Disclosing Party as it would treat its own information of a similar nature, (ii) take all reasonable precautions not to disclose such Confidential Information ***Confidential Treatment Requested*** - 51 - to Third Parties, without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent, and (iii) not use such Confidential Information other than for fulfilling its obligations or exercising its rights under this Agreement. If any Confidential Information is required to be disclosed by the Receiving Party or its Affiliates to comply with a court or administrative order, the Receiving Party or its Affiliates, prior to making such disclosure, shall furnish as much notice as is reasonable under the circumstances to the Disclosing Party to enable it to resist such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information' within the exclusion clause of the Confidential Information definition (1.17). This creates a contradiction with section 16.1, which mandates specific treatment for 'Confidential Information.' The change introduces ambiguity as to whether the exclusions defined in 1.17 apply to all Confidential Information, or only to some subset now labeled 'Proprietary Information'. This directly impacts the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations in 16.1.

perturbation.original_text: 3.4.1 Scope Roche and FMI shall conduct the CDx Development Program pursuant to a mutually agreed R&D Plan. The activities conducted in connection with the CDx Development Program will be overseen by the JRDC. The purpose of the CDx Development Program is to develop certain companion diagnostic tests or assays (the "CDx Assays") for use in connection with certain Roche products. Such CDx Assays may include those developed at Roche's request in connection with markers that have not yet been approved by the FDA for the particular tumor type/indication for which Roche is developing the relevant therapeutic ("Investigational Markers") and those developed by mutual agreement of the Parties in connection with markers that are included in one or more assays approved by the FDA for the particular tumor type/indication to indicate use of a Roche therapeutic ("Approved Markers").
perturbation.changed_text: 3.4.1 Scope Roche and FMI shall conduct the CDx Development Program pursuant to a mutually agreed R&D Plan. The activities conducted in connection with the CDx Development initiative will be overseen by the JRDC. The purpose of the CDx Development initiative is to develop certain companion diagnostic tests or assays (the "CDx Assays") for use in connection with certain Roche products. Such CDx Assays may include those developed at Roche's request in connection with markers that have not yet been approved by the FDA for the particular tumor type/indication for which Roche is developing the relevant therapeutic ("Investigational Markers") and those developed by mutual agreement of the Parties in connection with markers that are included in one or more assays approved by the FDA for the particular tumor type/indication to indicate use of a Roche therapeutic ("Approved Markers").
perturbation.explanation: By changing "CDx Development Program" to "CDx Development initiative", this contract creates confusion, it is now unclear if the parties obligations extend also to the CDx Development initiative, given the definition set forward for CDx Development Program, that is used across all text. This leads to uncertainty in enforcement regarding the scope of obligations and deliverables.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4.3 R&D Plan The Parties will conduct the CDx Development Program in accordance with the R&D Plan and in compliance with Performance Specifications and Quality Standards. Unless decided otherwise by the JMC, the R&D Plan will be updated […***…] by the CDx Platform Working Group, reviewed and recommended for approval by the JRDC and approved by the JMC. The R&D Plan will set forth (i) the scope of the CDx Development Program and the resources that will be dedicated to the activities contemplated within the CDx Development Program, including the responsibilities of each Party (ii) specific objectives for each year, which objectives will be updated or amended, as appropriate, by the JRDC as research progresses, and (iii) budgets for such activities. The Parties shall prepare a plan for activities to be conducted no later than […***…] before the first anniversary of the Effective Date. The JMC shall review the R&D Plan on an ongoing basis and may amend the R&D Plan. Any such changes shall be reflected in written amendments to the R&D Plan.
perturbation.justification: The change from "CDx Development Program" to "CDx Development initiative" introduces ambiguity. The contradicted text (3.4.3) explicitly references and defines obligations related to the "CDx Development Program". By using a different term, the changed text creates uncertainty as to whether the obligations outlined in section 3.4.3 apply to the "CDx Development initiative", leading to a potential contradiction and uncertainty in enforcement as described in the perturbation explanation.
